_comment: Canonical world graph for The Legend of Tux. Run tools/build_zones.py to regenerate stub .tscn files. The slice is fully populated; later zones are stubs.

sapling_blade.description: A practice sword carved from a young sapling.

bark_round.description: A round shield woven from inner bark.

wyrdwood_hollow._layout: 60×60m clearing. North is -Z. The playable bowl is bounded by a tall perimeter ridge (no walking off the world). A dirt path runs spawn→dungeon along the central N-S axis. Three cottages ring the clearing. A pond sits in the SW corner.

terrains._role: outer ridge — sloped wall around the playable bowl. Shaded so the elevation actually reads as hills.
inner floor — the actual clearing, flat at Y≈0, deliberately small so the player feels enclosed.
dirt path running spawn→dungeon along the central axis. Slightly raised so it z-fights cleanly.

wyrdwood_hollow._tree_ring: Dense tree perimeter inside the ridge — visual + collision wall.
wyrdwood_hollow._cottage_block: Three cottages around the clearing. NW and NE flank the path north toward the dungeon; SW sits beside the pond. The rot_y in degrees rotates the cottage so the door faces the clearing.

cottages._role: SW — Old Hennra, by the pond

wyrdwood_hollow._water: A small pond in the SW corner. The water_volume scene wraps a translucent blue surface with a damage trigger off by default.

chests.open_message: A practice sword. Brann said you'd know it when you saw it.
A round shield of woven bark — light, but it'll hold.

wyrdwood_hollow._tutorial_signs: Cluster at spawn so the player reads them on arrival. Each one is one mechanic. Faced toward the player (rot_y=180 since spawn is to the south looking north, signs at the south edge face south back at the player).

signs.message:
MOVE

WASD to walk.
Mouse to look around.
SPACE to jump.

INTERACT

E — talk to people, open chests, read signs.
Look at a thing first and the prompt will show.

FIGHT

Once you have the Sapling Blade:
LEFT-CLICK to swing.
RIGHT-CLICK to raise the shield (after you find one).

EDIT

TAB to enter the in-game editor at any time.
Click things, drag points, tweak. TAB again to return.

ELDER PILLAR

The pillar's heartwood will not open
until you carry both blade and shield.

wyrdwood_hollow._npcs: Five villagers. Each has 1 line of dialog directed at the player. Body/hat colors set the silhouette so the user can recognize them at a glance.

npcs.name: Brann the Elder
npcs.dialog_text: 'Two chests in the clearing — blade and shield. Open both, then north. The pillar won't have you unarmed.'

npcs.name: Vesta the Whittler
npcs.dialog_text: 'I carved that little blade myself. It'll bend before it breaks. So would I, mind.'

npcs.name: Maelin the Tinker
npcs.dialog_text: 'The bark round is light because hands tire. Heavier is not always better. Tell Brann I said so.'

npcs.name: Old Hennra
npcs.dialog_text: 'The pond's mine. I sit by it. There are pebbles in it. You may take a few. The water won't notice.'

npcs.name: Wisp-Tender
npcs.dialog_text: 'Glim follows you because she likes the look of you. Don't disappoint her.'

wyrdwood_hollow._pebbles: Scattered along the path and around the pond so the player picks them up walking the route.

load_zones.gate_message: The Elder Pillar's heartwood refuses you. You feel that without both blade and shield, it will not open.

elder_pillar_interior._layout: Three connected chambers running south (entry) to north (boss). Wall_segments make the divisions. Torches mark room corners.

terrains._role: Dark wooden floor for the whole interior.

elder_pillar_interior._walls: Two staggered dividers create a slalom: south room (entry), middle room, north room (boss). Each gap is offset so the player has to move across the chamber to find it. Outer perimeter walls close the chamber so the player can't walk out the side.

walls._role: south wall around the entry, gap centered on the door
second divider — gap on west, forces slalom

signs.message:
INSIDE THE ELDER PILLAR

The heartwood thrums.
Something nests deeper in. Bring it down.
Combat: LEFT-CLICK swings the Sapling Blade. RIGHT-CLICK raises the Bark Round.

Something larger waits ahead.
Keep the shield up.

chests.open_message: A heart container — the Elder Pillar's blessing. You feel hardier.

_future_zones._comment: Stubs for later passes — not generated yet.
